description: Machine-readable status source for public EconCSLib paper formalizations. Markdown tables, the site status table, and release notes should be synchronized from this file.
review_count_policy: reviewed_rows counts saved human dashboard rows tracked in the public repository. total_rows counts the current dashboard review surface, after review_slices.json filtering when present. Agent source audits are not counted as human review.
paper_interface_maintenance_policy: PaperInterface.lean should stay compact and source-facing. Oversized interfaces or large filtered dashboards are tracked as maintainability debt.

papers.title: Addressing Discretization-Induced Bias in Demographic Prediction
papers.authors: Evan Dong, Aaron Schein, Yixin Wang, and Nikhil Garg
papers.main_caveat: Main theorem route differs from the paper proof in places; the validation report records the proof-route deviation.

human_review.source: PaperInterface declaration count; no tracked public dashboard log

papers.title: Competitive Auctions and Digital Goods
papers.authors: Andrew V. Goldberg, Jason D. Hartline, and Andrew Wright
papers.source_version: SODA 2001 / InterTrust numbering crosswalk; journal version controls refinements
papers.main_caveat: Theorem 8.2 is formalized against the journal version's monotone truthful randomized-auction statement; Lean refutes the broader technical-report wording with a two-bidder 101 > 100 source-audit counterexample. Theorem 9.3 is closed against the paper's focused set-of-bids deterministic auction convention.

human_review.source: FINAL_VALIDATION_REPORT.md dashboard status; no tracked public human review log

papers.title: User-item fairness tradeoffs in recommendations
papers.authors: Sophie Greenwood, Sudalakshmee Chiniah, and Nikhil Garg
papers.main_caveat: Recommendation fairness propositions and theorem statements are closed under the formal source model.

human_review.source: PaperInterface declaration count; no tracked public dashboard log

papers.title: The Economics of Matching: Stability and Incentives
papers.main_caveat: The cached paper's named Theorems 1--7, Lemmas 1--2, and Corollary 5.1 are closed on Roth's stated strict marriage domain without extra model-certificate assumptions.

human_review.source: FINAL_VALIDATION_REPORT.md says ignored local dashboard logs contain stale older entries; no tracked public human review log is counted

papers.title: College Admissions and the Stability of Marriage
papers.authors: D. Gale and L. S. Shapley
papers.main_caveat: Lean endpoints are closed without extra certificates or conditional proof gaps; the cached PDF scan has poor OCR, recorded as a source-audit note rather than a formalization caveat.

human_review.source: PaperInterface declaration count; no tracked public dashboard log

papers.title: Balancing Producer Fairness and Efficiency via Bayesian Rating System Design
papers.authors: Thomas Ma, Michael S. Bernstein, Ramesh Johari, and Nikhil Garg
papers.main_caveat: Bayesian rating-system results are formalized with a documented boundary correction.

human_review.source: PaperInterface declaration count; no tracked public dashboard log

papers.title: AdWords and Generalized Online Matching
papers.authors: Aranyak Mehta, Amin Saberi, Umesh Vazirani, and Vijay V. Vazirani
papers.main_caveat: Core AdWords, Theorems 8--9, and Section 6/8 extensions are closed, including a source-shaped page-level guarantee.

papers.title: Test-optional Policies: Overcoming Strategic Behavior and Informational Gaps
papers.main_caveat: All named definitions and Section 3--4 results are closed under paper-facing source models; dashboard review remains external.

papers.title: Driver Surge Pricing
papers.main_caveat: Named CTMC lemmas and Theorems 1--4 are exposed; zero-mass boundary behavior is audited separately.

papers.title: On Approximately Fair Allocations of Indivisible Goods
papers.authors: Richard J. Lipton, Evangelos Markakis, Elchanan Mossel, and Amin Saberi
papers.main_caveat: Public partial: Sections 2 and 4 are formalized and much of the Section 3 rounding/search surface is formalized. The final PTAS/FPTAS runtime claim is conditional on reusable fixed-dimension integer-program complexity infrastructure, represented by ExternalSolverConsequence.

human_review.source: FINAL_VALIDATION_REPORT.md and review_slices.json filtered dashboard surface

paper_interface.maintainability_issue: PaperInterface.lean is oversized and relies on review_slices.json to filter 791 declarations down to 33 source-facing rows; shrink toward paper-facing definitions and named results.

papers.title: Truth Revelation in Approximately Efficient Combinatorial Auctions
papers.authors: Daniel Lehmann, Liadan Ita O'Callaghan, and Yoav Shoham
papers.main_caveat: Public partial: all auction, greedy approximation, critical-price, and single-minded-truthfulness endpoints are formalized in the current model. The final Theorem 6.1 machine-level NP-hardness/NP = ZPP consequences remain conditional on reusable complexity-class and hardness infrastructure.

human_review.source: FINAL_VALIDATION_REPORT.md and review_slices.json filtered dashboard surface

paper_interface.maintainability_issue: PaperInterface.lean is oversized and relies on review_slices.json to filter 224 declarations down to 30 source-facing rows; shrink toward paper-facing definitions and named results.
